top_signals.title: Trump keeps saying an Iran deal is close. Markets keep believing it
top_signals.summary: Analysts still assume that, despite the continued conflict, a deal will eventually be reached that ends the war and reopens the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: U.S. military completes strikes on 'multiple targets' in Iran launched at Trump's direction
top_signals.summary: President Donald Trump promised more attacks on Iran after strikes resumed this week. Earlier this week, he said a deal to end the war was near.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: From Pyongyang to Primorsk: When Sanctions Evasion Becomes System Design
top_signals.summary:
Rarely a week passes without a new story about Russia’s shadow fleet. Tankers catch fire in the Mediterranean, are added to sanctions lists, or are boarded while passing through European waters. But the bigger story is not the vessels that are caught, but those that aren’t — ships moving between registries, ports, shell companies, and service providers that obscure their ties to Russia while keeping a sanctioned state afloat. The vessels that do get sanctioned are the visible tip of a larger scheme that North Korea spent years running, and Russia has refined at scale.Shadow fleets are typically studied in

The post From Pyongyang to Primorsk: When Sanctions Evasion Becomes System Design appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: White House defends Cuba sanctions as U.N. warns of harm
top_signals.summary: U.N. ⁠High Commissioner for ​Human Rights Volker Turk said the measures were affecting the Cuban population's human rights, especially ​access to ‌essential supplies and services.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Thai Long Bonds Draw Funds With Steepest Curve in Emerging Asia
top_signals.summary: Thailand’s yield curve has shifted to the steepest in emerging Asia, with some investors saying that makes its longer-dated bonds look attractive given a divergence in interest-rate expectations there versus regional peers.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Danantara Starts Bond Sale in Test After Indonesian Selloff
top_signals.summary: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund Danantara kicked off its debut global dollar bond sale, testing investor appetite at a time when confidence in Southeast Asia’s largest economy has soured.

top_signals.title: Canada moves to ban social media for children under 16 and regulate AI chatbots
top_signals.summary: Companies could face penalties of 3% ​of global revenue or up to C$10 million ($7.2 million), whichever is more, for failing to comply.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: GitHub announces npm security changes to tackle supply-chain attacks
top_signals.summary: GitHub has announced that npm v12, expected next month, will introduce several security-focused changes aimed at blocking supply-chain attacks abusing behaviors triggered by the 'npm install' command. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Injured Takumi Minamino takes on role of mentor for Japan at World Cup
top_signals.summary: The Japanese ​international ruptured the anterior cruciate ligament in his left knee while playing for AS Monaco in December
